key_items: Bitcoin's major holders halt buys as demand slows - CryptoQuant warns of deteriorating holding structure preceding sustained weakness
Bitcoin trapped under $74K with $9B options expiry looms - Bears in control with heavy ETF outflows and corporate selling pressure
Bitcoin slips below $74k for first time since April - Market recovery lost momentum with $1B in crypto liquidations across derivatives
reasoning: Multiple bearish on-chain signals (major holder capitulation, deteriorating momentum) combined with technical breakdown below $74K and significant ETF outflows create near-term downward pressure. Geopolitical tensions (US-Iran), $9B options expiry, and weak derivative positioning suggest 1-7 day weakness before potential stabilization.
risks: Breakdown below critical $72K support could cascade into $60K retest zone as bearish technicals align with potential forced liquidations in overleveraged positions.
